app.name: OpenTraderWorld
search.deep: Also search content titles (Editor, Goals, Calendar, ToDo, Routines, Reminders, Prompts, Community Docs)
dashboard.title: Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Your activated modules, arranged your way.
dashboard.empty.title: No modules yet
dashboard.empty.body: When modules are installed and activated, they show up here as tiles.
dashboard.pageTagHint: Short label shown in the page switcher.
status.title: Services
setup.title: Welcome to OpenTraderWorld
setup.subtitle: Create your admin account to finish setup.
setup.error.short: Password must be at least 8 characters.
login.title: Sign in
changePassword.title: Set a new password
changePassword.intro: This account uses a temporary password from the installer. Choose your own password to continue.
changePassword.err.tooShort: New password must be at least 8 characters.
settings.title: Settings
settings.defaults.title: Defaults
settings.defaults.subtitle: Used as the starting currency and timezone across modules.
settings.defaults.languageHint: Applies to the whole app immediately.
settings.account.title: Account
settings.account.revokeNote: Changing your password signs you out of all sessions.
settings.account.err.currentRequired: Enter your current password to confirm.
settings.network.title: Network
settings.network.subtitle: Controls who can reach this app. Changes take effect after a restart (command shown below).
settings.network.mode.localHint: Reachable only from this machine. Safest — nothing on the network can connect.
settings.network.mode.lanHint: Reachable by other devices on your network via this machine’s IP. Plain HTTP.
settings.network.mode.lanhttpsHint: Reachable on your network over HTTPS with a real certificate — no browser warnings on any device. Nothing is exposed to the internet.
settings.network.lanHttpsHelp: Uses a free Let's Encrypt certificate obtained through your DNS provider (DNS challenge), so it works on a private network without opening any port. DuckDNS is free: sign in at duckdns.org, create a subdomain, and paste its token below.
settings.network.dnsTokenHint: DuckDNS: the token shown on your duckdns.org account page. Cloudflare: an API token with DNS edit permission for this zone.
settings.network.cfDomainHint: In your Cloudflare zone, create an A record for this name pointing at this machine's LAN IP.
settings.network.lanIpHint: The DuckDNS record is pointed at this private IP automatically. Correct the prefill if it doesn't match this machine.
settings.network.ctNote: Note: issued certificates are public (Certificate Transparency), so the chosen domain name is publicly visible. The app itself stays LAN-only.
settings.network.err.dnsTokenRequired: A DNS provider token is required.
settings.network.err.lanIpRequired: This machine's LAN IP is required.
settings.network.mode.webHint: Exposed on a domain with automatic HTTPS. Only enable if this host is internet-facing.
settings.network.domainHint: Point this domain’s DNS at this host; Caddy fetches a TLS cert automatically. Served on 443.
settings.network.err.domainRequired: A domain is required for this mode.
settings.network.restartBody: Settings saved. Apply them by running this on the host:
settings.network.restartNote: The app briefly goes offline while containers recreate.
settings.modules.title: Modules
settings.modules.subtitle: Install a module to make it available in the switcher and on the dashboard. Detaching hides it and makes it inaccessible; you may also delete its stored data. Nothing is downloaded — every module ships with the app.
settings.modules.detachConfirmBody: {name} will be hidden from the switcher and dashboard and become inaccessible. You can reinstall it any time.
settings.data.title: Manage data
settings.data.subtitle: Size includes tables, indexes and large-value storage. Wiping a module permanently deletes its data and cannot be undone.
settings.data.wipeConfirmBody: This permanently deletes all {name} data ({rows} rows). Type {name} to confirm.
settings.backup.title: Backup database
settings.backup.intro: Run this on the host where the stack is deployed. It dumps the whole database over the existing Postgres connection — no extra access required.
settings.backup.encryptBody: A dump contains your data in the clear. Pipe it through gpg (or age) so the file is encrypted on disk; you'll be prompted for a passphrase.
settings.backup.security1: API keys/credentials (news feeds, data providers) are already encrypted at rest with OTW_SECRET_KEY, so they appear only as ciphertext in the dump. The backup is useless for those without that key.
settings.backup.security2: Back up OTW_SECRET_KEY (from your .env) separately — not inside the same dump — or encrypted secrets can't be restored.
settings.backup.security3: The dump includes live sessions tokens. Treat the file as a secret, or drop that table after restoring and sign in again.
settings.backup.security4: Store the encrypted backup off-box and rotate older copies.
settings.update.title: Update app
settings.update.intro: Updating rebuilds the containers, which the app can't do to itself by design — it runs without shell or Docker access to keep the attack surface small. Run these on the host:
settings.update.before1: Take a database backup first (see the Backup section).
settings.update.before2: Migrations run automatically on the new container's first boot.
settings.update.before3: Review release notes for any breaking changes.
settings.update.checkFailed: Couldn't check for updates (no connection to GitHub).
settings.logs.title: Logs
settings.logs.subtitle: Sets the minimum severity written to storage (takes effect immediately). Lower levels capture more detail and use more space.
settings.rate.title: API rate tracking
settings.rate.subtitle: Outbound calls to external data providers (market data, FX, quotes, feeds), counted since the start of the UTC day. Nothing here throttles or blocks requests — it only tracks volume and flags providers that answered with a rate-limit response.
settings.rate.noData: No outbound API calls recorded in this window yet.
settings.mcp.title: MCP
settings.mcp.subtitle: Lets AI agents (any MCP-compatible client) use this app through a controlled gateway. Each token decides which modules an agent can read or change; everything else stays out of reach. Account, network, secrets and data-wipe operations are never exposed.
settings.mcp.offNote: The MCP endpoint is off. You can prepare tokens below, but every agent request is rejected until you enable it.
settings.mcp.tokensHint: One token per agent or use case. The token is stored hashed and shown only once; revoke it any time.
settings.mcp.name: Name
settings.mcp.createdBody: Copy the token now — it is shown only once and stored hashed.
settings.mcp.connectHint: The endpoint speaks MCP over Streamable HTTP, so any compliant client works. Authenticate with a Bearer token created above; replace <TOKEN> in the snippet.
settings.mcp.note1: Agents discover what they may call via the otw_catalog tool — only the modules you granted appear.
settings.mcp.note2: Writes require the module’s read+write level; reads require at least read.
settings.mcp.note3: If the app is reachable only on localhost, agents must run on this machine too.
settings.credits.title: Credits
settings.credits.subtitle: Data sources and upstream projects the platform can use — including providers available in the app that you haven't configured. Thanks to their authors.
settings.about.title: About
settings.about.subtitle: OpenTraderWorld is free, open source and self-hosted. Useful links:
settings.about.shareBody: Sharing it with a friend or your community is the best way to support the project.
settings.about.shareText: I'm using OpenTraderWorld, a free self-hosted platform for traders and investors. Check it out:
backtest.page.expertHint: Open the full-screen strategy & indicator builder
backtest.expert.title: Backtest — Expert
backtest.ind.outputTitle: Use this step as the indicator output
backtest.ind.hint: Each step can reference earlier steps only. The highlighted step is the output.
backtest.ind.hintV2: Each step applies an indicator to a source (a price or an earlier step), or computes a formula. The highlighted steps are the outputs.
backtest.trades.maeTitle: Max adverse excursion — worst open loss while in the trade
backtest.trades.mfeTitle: Max favorable excursion — best open profit while in the trade
backtest.signalGroup.toggleLogicTitle: Click to switch — ALL rules must hold (AND) vs any one is enough (OR)
backtest.side.noExitRulesHint: No exit rules — exits via SL/TP or reversal.
backtest.side.exitOnReverseHint: Exit when entry conditions no longer hold
backtest.settings.reverseSideHint: Reverse side — short = inverse of long (skip configuring short)
backtest.settings.stopAndReverseHint: Stop & reverse — flip position when the opposite signal fires
backtest.settings.shortDerivedNote: Short side derived as the inverse of long.
backtest.settings.pyramidingTitle: Max stacked entries per position (1 = off). Re-fires of the entry signal add size.
backtest.settings.pyramidingNote: Up to {count} stacked entries; SL/TP track the average entry price.
backtest.settings.riskNeedsStop: Risk-based sizing needs a stop-loss on the traded side(s).
backtest.settings.tierNote: Highest tier whose threshold ≤ current equity wins. Thresholds must increase.
backtest.settings.kellyNote: Fractional Kelly from the last N closed trades; capped, floored at 0. Warm-up sizing until the window fills.
backtest.settings.limitsNote: Blank = no limit. Exposure is total open notional vs equity.
backtest.adv.oosNote: Runs the strategy on both halves and shows the two stat blocks side by side.
backtest.adv.fundingNote: Constant funding on open notional — longs pay a positive rate, shorts receive. Estimate only.
backtest.grid.note: A ladder of levels; each cell buys low and sells at the next level up. Qty per level, or split the total budget across cells.
backtest.chart.zoomHint: Zoom in to see individual trades
backtest.page.datasetDeletedErr: Source dataset was deleted — pick another to rerun.
backtest.page.title: Backtest
backtest.page.tagline: Combine indicator signals, size with pyramiding, measure the edge.
backtest.oos.title: In-sample vs out-of-sample
backtest.oos.note: A large gap between the two columns is a sign of overfitting.
backtest.align.title: Alignment
backtest.align.noOverlap: No overlapping bars — these datasets never trade together.
backtest.asset.title: Per-asset breakdown
backtest.page.emptyHint: Pick a dataset, stack entry & exit conditions, then run.
backtest.page.noSavedRuns: No runs yet — every backtest you run lands here automatically.
backtest.page.historyHint: Every run is kept here (last 300). Named runs are starred and never removed.
backtest.page.clearHistoryConfirm: Clear the run history? Named runs are kept.
backtest.page.name: Name
histdata.providers.vaultHint: API keys plug in from the central vault — store them once, reuse them across connectors.
histdata.connectors.name: Name
histdata.connectors.none: No connectors yet. Add one to start downloading.
histdata.connectors.deleteMessage: Delete “{name}”? Its credentials and usage tracking are removed; downloaded datasets are kept.
histdata.connectors.errMax: Enter a request limit of at least 1, or tick Unlimited.
histdata.datasets.emptyNone: No datasets stored yet. Download some history first.
histdata.datasets.fetchNewerTitle: Fetch bars newer than the last one stored
histdata.datasets.deleteConfirmBody:
Delete {provider} {ticker} {timeframe}?
All bars are dropped and this can’t be undone.
histdata.download.needsCredentials: Needs credentials — set them in Settings
histdata.download.errOptionFields: Enter the underlying, expiry, type and a valid strike.
histdata.download.optIncomplete: Fill the fields to build the contract symbol
histdata.download.futureHint: Contract code: base + month letter + year digit (F G H J K M N Q U V X Z = Jan…Dec).
histdata.download.missingSecrets: {label} needs credentials ({secrets}) — set them in Settings.
histdata.download.outsideLimit: This provider serves {timeframe} data only for the last {days} days — your “From” date is older.
histdata.download.limitsModalIntro: This provider only serves intraday data for a limited window before today. Data older than the limit isn’t available at any granularity below daily — shorten the range, or use the 1d / 1w timeframe (no limit) for older history.
histdata.page.title: Historical Data
histviz.page.title: Visualization
histviz.panel.title: Indicators
histviz.picker.searchHint: Search or filter to find a dataset.
histviz.settings.title: Chart settings
mportfolios.title: Managers' Portfolios
mportfolios.snapshots.deleteGroupTitle: Delete all snapshots for this portfolio
mportfolios.snapshots.empty: No snapshots yet. Use the snapshot button in the Portfolios tab to save one.
mportfolios.detail.title: Portfolio
mportfolios.detail.saveSnapshotTitle: Save a snapshot of this portfolio
portfolios.addAsset.title: Add asset
portfolios.addAsset.currencyHint: currency your buys/sells are entered in
portfolios.alloc.title: Allocation
portfolios.alloc.empty: Nothing held yet — allocation appears once assets have value.
portfolios.valueChart.notEnoughHistory: Not enough history yet — refresh on a few different days to chart value.
portfolios.reconcile.title: Set up auto-refresh
portfolios.reconcile.lead: Each holding is checked against its price source. Resolve any that come up unresolved (fix the source, or mark it manual) — then auto-refresh prices them daily in the background.
portfolios.reconcile.noAssets: No assets to reconcile — add one first.
portfolios.detail.refreshCooldown: Prices were just refreshed — wait a minute before refreshing again.
portfolios.detail.sourcesInfo.title: Where prices come from
portfolios.detail.sourcesInfo.coingecko: crypto spot prices and symbol search (keyless public API).
portfolios.detail.sourcesInfo.yahoo: stocks and ETFs — last close or live quote, plus symbol search.
portfolios.detail.sourcesInfo.exchanges: last trade price, when an asset overrides its source to a specific exchange.
portfolios.detail.sourcesInfo.limits: All sources are free, keyless public endpoints with per-IP rate limits (CoinGecko ~10–30 calls/min; Kraken ~1/sec). One refresh calls every source your assets use.
portfolios.detail.sourcesInfo.warn: Nothing throttles you here: if a provider rate-limits you, the affected assets are silently skipped and keep their previous price — the refresh still looks successful. A few refreshes a day is plenty; prices are snapshotted daily anyway.
portfolios.detail.unresolvedHint: No price from its source — set up auto-refresh to fix
portfolios.detail.noAssetsYet: No assets yet. Add one to start tracking.
portfolios.detail.confirmDeleteAsset: Delete this asset and its operations?
portfolios.page.title: Portfolio Tracker
portfolios.card.confirmDelete: Delete this portfolio and all its data?
portfolios.card.emptyState: No portfolios yet. Create one to start tracking.
watchlists.page.title: Watchlists
watchlists.page.emptyState: Keep an eye on the symbols you care about — live price, day change and a 30-day trend per symbol.
watchlists.new.title: New watchlist
watchlists.new.templateHint: Start from a curated list — symbols are added and priced right away.
watchlists.new.portfolioHint: Mirror a portfolio's assets. Importing again later reconciles instead of duplicating.
watchlists.new.noPortfolios: No portfolios yet — create one in the Portfolio Tracker first.
watchlists.add.title: Add symbol
watchlists.detail.rateWarning: High request rate — free price sources may throttle. Choose a slower interval or trim the list.
watchlists.detail.confirmDelete: Delete this watchlist and all its symbols? This cannot be undone.
watchlists.detail.emptyItems: No symbols yet. Add one to start tracking prices.
watchlists.page.sourcesHint: Watchlists' own provider accounts (separate from Historical Data). Create a connector, then enter its API key in the right-hand panel; pin it as a list's default source or per symbol and unlock faster refresh intervals.
watchlists.detail.customProviderWarn: Custom source — know your API plan's limits: excess calls fail and can get your key blocked. Track usage in Settings → API rate tracking.
quant.kelly.note: Full Kelly maximizes long-run growth but is volatile; most traders size at half or quarter Kelly to cut drawdowns.
quant.metrics.conditionalVarHint: Average loss on the catastrophic days beyond VaR.
quant.metrics.historicalVolatilityHint: Annualized swing — how wide stops must be to survive normal noise.
quant.metrics.maxDrawdownHint: Worst peak-to-trough drop on record. Could you stomach it?
quant.metrics.valueAtRiskHint: Historical loss boundary of a bad period (parametric −{param}).
quant.page.correlationHint: Are you diversifying, or buying the same asset twice?
quant.page.frontierHint: Each dot is a random allocation — click a highlighted point for its weights.
quant.page.noDatasetsHint: No datasets found. Download data in the Historical Data module first.
quant.page.pickAndAnalyzeHint: Pick a dataset and analyze its risk profile.
quant.page.pickDatasetForRange: Pick a dataset to choose a range.
quant.page.riskParityHint: Sizes volatile assets smaller so no single name dominates risk.
quant.page.selectDatasetsHint: Select datasets to compare and analyze the portfolio.
quant.page.title: Quant Tools
quant.seasonality.pickHint: Pick a dataset and analyze to see its month, weekday and (intraday) hour seasonality.
quant.mc.sourceRunHint: Resamples a saved backtest run's realized trades. The run is replayed to regenerate its exact trade sequence.
quant.mc.noRunsHint: No saved backtest runs yet. Run and save a backtest first, then come back here.
quant.mc.ruinThresholdHint: A path is “ruined” if equity ever falls to this fraction of the starting capital.
quant.mc.horizonHint: Trades per simulated path. Blank uses the run's own trade count.
quant.mc.pickRunHint: Pick a saved backtest run and run the simulation to see drawdown and risk-of-ruin bands.
quant.mc.riskOfRuinHint: Share of paths whose equity ever hit the ruin threshold.
quant.mc.medianMaxDdHint: Half of paths drew down more than this; the p95 path reached −{p95}.
quant.mc.probLossHint: Share of paths ending below the starting capital.
quant.mc.equityFanHint: Percentile bands (p5–p95, p25–p75) of the resampled equity paths, with the median and the actual realized curve.
quant.mc.distributionsHint: Final equity and worst drawdown across all simulated paths.
quant.size.entryMismatch: Entry is far from the asset's price — stops scale to your entry. Click “Use last as entry” for stops on the real price.
quant.size.noDatasets: No datasets — download data in Historical Data first.
quant.size.suggestStopBody: Load a downloaded dataset to get volatility-, ATR-, and swing-based stops for your entry. Click one to fill the Stop field.
quant.size.suggestStopTitle: Suggest a stop from an asset
wealth.assetForm.name: Name
wealth.netWorthChart.empty: No data yet — add an asset and record a value.
wealth.templates.name: Name
wealth.templates.addFieldsHint: Add fields above. Price & quantity feed the asset's value; custom fields are free-form notes per revision.

wealth.import.categoryHint: Assets are added with category “{name}”, valued at today's price.

routines.page.emptyBoardHint: Routines are recurring checklists — pre-market prep, in-session discipline, post-market review — due on the weekdays you pick.

journal.pending.intro: FX rates auto-fetch. Days here couldn't be fetched — trades on them are excluded from the converted total until you add rates by hand. Rates are USD-based: enter how much of each currency one US dollar buys.

news.feedForm.name: Name
news.feedForm.vaultTip: For API keys, always use the Vault: reference a stored secret inline as {{vaultname.itemname}} — e.g. {{AlphaVantage.apikey}} — in the endpoint, a header, or a query value. It's resolved from your Vault at fetch time and scrubbed from logs. Never paste a raw key here — a pasted value can appear in clear text in logs and leak.
news.feedForm.saveFirstHint: Save the feed first, then add API keys/secrets to it.
news.feedForm.dupMsgIn: An identical source already exists in: {names}.
news.feedForm.dupQuestion: Reuse the same source so its items aren't fetched twice, or create a separate copy?
news.feedForm.rateLimitHint: (one request per poll — usage shows on the source card)

Delete dashboard "{name}"?
Its sources are kept (unlinked), not deleted.

findb.gate.importing: Importing catalog… this runs in the background and takes a minute.
findb.gate.installHint: The catalog is downloaded and imported once (~15 MB). It then lives locally — searches never touch the network.
findb.search.placeholder: Search symbol or name… (e.g. AAPL, Vanguard, BTC)
findb.search.hint: Type a symbol or name, or pick a filter to browse the catalog.

taxcalc.loadJournal.title: Load Trading Journal
taxcalc.loadJournal.summarySingular: 1 closed trade in {year} · realized net of {fees} fees · figures in {currency}
taxcalc.loadJournal.summaryPlural: {count} closed trades in {year} · realized net of {fees} fees · figures in {currency}
taxcalc.loadJournal.unconvertedSingular: 1 closed trade excluded — FX rate unavailable for their date (resolve them in the Journal).
taxcalc.loadJournal.unconvertedPlural: {count} closed trades excluded — FX rate unavailable for their date (resolve them in the Journal).
taxcalc.loadJournal.applyHint: Switches the form to Itemized mode and fills these three fields. Adjust before computing.
taxcalc.loadJournal.noFxRate: No FX rate found to convert {from}→{to}; figures left in {from}.

taxcalc.profile.personTypeHint: Switch the country template to autofill rates for individual/pro.
taxcalc.profile.flatRate: Flat rate on gains % (blank = template default)
taxcalc.profile.noWealthTax: No wealth tax. Add brackets to tax total portfolio value (e.g. CH, ES, NO).
taxcalc.profile.marginalBracketHint: Marginal: each rate applies between the previous cap and its "up to". Leave the last blank for the top bracket.
taxcalc.page.pickProfileFirst: Pick or create a profile first.
taxcalc.page.journalNoteMismatch: Loaded from journal — figures are in {currency} but your profile is {profileCurrency} (no FX rate was available to convert). Adjust manually.
taxcalc.page.journalNoteLoaded: Loaded realized PnL from the Trading Journal ({currency}).
taxcalc.page.title: Tax Calculator

time.resume.body: A timer kept running while the app was closed. Keep that time, or revert to when you last had the app open ({time})?
time.empty: No projects yet. Add one with the add button to start tracking time.
time.details.title: {name} — time ranges
time.details.noRanges: No ranges yet — start the timer or add one above.
time.details.errDurationPositive: Enter a duration greater than zero.
time.details.confirmDeleteRange: Delete this time range? This can't be undone.
time.confirmDeleteBody: Delete project {name} and all its time entries? This can't be undone.

time.chart.empty: No tracked time in this range.

remindme.notifs.title: Notifications
remindme.notifs.empty: No notifications yet. Reminders will appear here when they fire.
remindme.channels.title: Notification channels
remindme.channels.intro: Also send reminders to your own email, Telegram, Slack or Discord. You bring the account; it stays yours. Only enabled channels receive notifications.
remindme.channels.empty: No channels yet. Add one below to also get reminders outside the app.

remindme.channels.secretRequired: A credential is required to create this channel.
remindme.channels.needSecretFirst: Add a credential before enabling this channel.

promptStore.history.confirmRollback: Roll back to version {version}? This saves it as a new version.

settings.logs.emptyHint: Activity is recorded here as the app runs.
settings.appearance.title: Appearance
settings.appearance.subtitle: Choose the app's primary color. Applies everywhere, instantly.
module.webhooks.desc: Inbound webhooks redirected to your modules.
module.agent.desc: Chat with an AI assistant using your own provider.
webhooks.title: Webhooks
webhooks.hint: Each webhook is a private URL. Give it to any service that can send an HTTP POST (an alerting platform, a monitor, a script…): every payload it receives is redirected to the module you chose — for now RemindMe: an in-app notification, also pushed to your enabled channels.
webhooks.netWarn: Network mode "{mode}" is not reachable from the internet, so external senders cannot deliver to this host.
webhooks.netWarnLink: Change the mode in Settings → Network
webhooks.netWarnOr: — or point a tunnel (e.g. Cloudflare Tunnel) at this host and keep the app private.
webhooks.emptyBody: Create one to turn incoming alerts into reminders.
webhooks.deleteBody: Delete webhook "{name}"? Its URL stops working immediately.
webhooks.name: Name
webhooks.createdBody: Copy this URL now — it embeds the secret token and is shown only once. Paste it wherever the sending service expects a webhook URL.
webhooks.setup1: Create the webhook here and copy its URL (shown once).
webhooks.setup2: Paste it into the sending service's webhook/notification URL field.
webhooks.setup3: The request body becomes the notification: plain text (first line = title), or JSON like {"title": "…", "message": "…"} — extra JSON fields are appended as lines.
webhooks.setup4: Your host must be reachable by the sender (Settings → Network).
journal.calendar.legend.hint: Click a day to see its trades
agent.confirm.title: Confirm
agent.chat.emptyReady: Type a message below to start the conversation.
agent.chat.emptyNotReady: Add a provider in settings to start chatting.
agent.chat.composerPh: Message the assistant…  (Enter to send, Shift+Enter for newline)
agent.chat.composerNotReady: Configure a provider in settings first…
agent.set.label: Label
agent.set.apiKeyKeepPh: Leave blank to keep the current key
agent.set.errBaseUrl: OpenAI-compatible providers need a base URL (e.g. https://openrouter.ai/api/v1).
agent.set.noProvidersDesc: Add a provider of your choice (OpenRouter, OpenAI, Anthropic, DeepSeek…) with its API key to start chatting.
agent.set.advancedHint: Extra request fields sent verbatim to the provider; a null value removes a key (e.g. {"stream_options": null}).
agent.set.advancedErr: Advanced parameters must be a JSON object.
agent.set.toolsHint: Default for new conversations — each conversation can switch its token from the chat header. The agent can read (and, if allowed below, write) only the modules the token grants.
agent.set.toolsHintNone: No MCP tokens yet — create one in Settings → AI agents to give the assistant access to your data.
agent.set.mcpOffNote: The global MCP server is off — that switch only governs network access for external agents. The built-in assistant runs in-process, so its tools keep working, limited to the selected token's modules.
agent.set.allowWritesDesc: Off = read-only. On = the agent may create/update/delete within the token's modules, automatically.
agent.set.writeWarn: Write mode: the agent can modify your data without asking
agent.mem.slugHintEdit: Slug is the key; create a new memory to change it.
agent.mem.descPh: One-line summary shown in the index
agent.mem.emptyDesc: The assistant will save durable facts here, or you can add your own.
agent.skl.name: Name
agent.skl.descPh: One line — always shown to the assistant
agent.skl.emptyDesc: Add reusable instructions the assistant can load on demand (e.g. a strategy-review checklist).
agent.pick.title: Provider & model
agent.pick.noModels: Couldn't load the model list — type a model name.
agent.pick.defaultTitle: Use this token for new conversations
agent.mcp.title: MCP servers
agent.mcp.subtitle: Connect the assistant to external platforms via their MCP servers.
agent.mcp.securityNote: External MCP tools see your conversation, and what they return is untrusted third-party content. Only add servers you trust, and be careful combining them with write access to your data.
agent.mcp.catalogNote: Curated, well-known servers (remote HTTP only, bring your own key). Verify the URL against the provider’s docs before adding.
agent.mcp.connect.title: Connect OpenTraderWorld
agent.mcp.connect.intro: Creates a token for this instance and enables the gateway. Read-only on every module by default — raise a module to write only if the agent needs it.
agent.mcp.connect.ready: Connected. The agent can now reach this instance.
agent.mcp.connect.once: This token is shown once. Copy it now — only a hash is stored.
agent.mcp.connect.willEnable: The MCP gateway is off; connecting turns it on.
agent.mcp.name: Name
agent.mcp.authKeepPh: Leave blank to keep the current value
agent.mcp.authHint: Sent as the header above on every request. Leave empty for servers without auth.
agent.mcp.errUrl: The URL must start with http(s)://.
agent.mcp.emptyDesc: Add one from the catalog below, or a custom server by URL.
agent.pick.extWarn: External tools + write access: injected content from a server could trigger changes to your data. Keep an eye on tool calls.
vault.title: Vault — API keys & secrets
vault.subtitle: One vault per external service (e.g. Binance), holding named keys like Binance.apikey and Binance.secretkey. Create as many vaults and keys as you need; modules plug keys in by reference instead of pasting values.
vault.trackingNote: Requests and rate limits are tracked per vault, not per key — every key of a vault counts toward the same counter.
vault.emptyHint: Create a vault to store API keys and secrets in one place.
vault.inUseNoDelete: Still plugged into a module — unplug it first
vault.note1: Values are write-only: once saved, a secret can never be viewed again — only replaced or deleted. Key names stay visible.
vault.note2: Deleting a vault or key that is still plugged into a module is blocked until you unplug it there.
vault.note3: The optional request limit is informational (observe-and-display) — nothing is throttled.
vault.name: Vault name
vault.itemsHint: Named values like apikey or secretkey. Values are sealed on save and can never be read back.
vault.limitToggle: Track requests with a rate limit
vault.limitHint: Counted vault-wide: any key used from this vault bumps the same counter. Displayed only, never enforced.
vault.err.dupItem: A key with this name already exists in this vault
vault.picker.empty: No vault keys yet — create a vault to reuse secrets across modules.
